published.text:
### Hero
Heading: AI Automation Consultant for Small Businesses
Body: An AI automation consultant for small business audits your day-to-day operations, identifies the manual steps that cost you the most time or lost revenue, and builds systems that handle those steps automatically — lead follow-up, scheduling, CRM workflows, and phone handling. Dmytro AI helps small businesses reduce management time by up to 15 hours per week and increase team capacity without hiring more staff. Fixed-price builds, typically $1.5K–$8K, with a working system live in 1–3 weeks.
Sub: Below: what a consultant actually does, how to tell if your business is a fit, what it costs, and how to evaluate your options.

### Core sections
- What an AI automation consultant actually does
- Signs your business is ready for automation
- Businesses that get the most from this
- What automation typically replaces
- Consultant vs. agency vs. DIY
- Good fit / not a fit
- Continue researching

### FAQ
Subhead: Straight answers about hiring an AI automation consultant
Topics: how consultant work differs from hiring a freelancer, how long results take, whether non-technical teams can use the system, what happens if you are not sure what to automate first, hidden costs, security/privacy, post-build support, and whether the systems work with existing tools.

### Final CTA
Heading: Book a 30-Minute Call
Body: Book a 30-minute call if you want to map the repetitive work that is costing time or revenue, understand whether a focused automation project is worth paying for now, and get a practical sense of scope, pricing, and next steps before committing to a build.

edits.description: This was an indexing/internal-linking refresh on a priority live money page, not a net-new GEO page ship.
Tightened the opening answer so the page states the buyer problem, consultant role, pricing band, and delivery timeline immediately instead of making the visitor infer them.

Added stronger contextual links to adjacent consultant-intent pages: what a consultant does, pricing, how to hire, consultant vs DIY, consultant vs agency, guides, best-first-automation, and case studies.
Kept proof disciplined by using only already-supported claims from the existing page and site-wide proof set.
Tone stays practical, specific, and non-hype: audit, bottlenecks, fixed-price builds, live in 1–3 weeks, and fit-based decision framing.
